item.title: The Brazilian Amazon Is the Next Battery Frontier
item.description: Going green deep in the rainforest.

item.title: US Wheat Crops Wither, Herds Thin as Spring Drought Deepens
item.description: Farmers across the Great Plains are confronting an intense drought that threatens winter wheat harvests and is pushing cattle producers toward costly feed purchases, prompting some to abandon plans to expand their herds.The dryness is expected to persist through spring after weeks of scant rainfall and a late-winter heat spell that fueled massive pasture fires across the nation's breadbasket. Drought now covers nearly 90% of Nebraska and Oklahoma, with more than half of Nebraska in "extreme" dro

item.title: Solar Panels and Batteries Are Changing Life in Brazil’s Amazon
item.description: Clean energy projects are taking off across the rainforest, reducing the need to burn diesel and bringing 24/7 power for refrigeration, schooling and tourism.

item.title: Amazon-Backed X-Energy Climbs 31% After $1.02 Billion US IPO
item.description: X-Energy Inc. shares opened 31% above their IPO price, after the nuclear energy firm raised $1.02 billion in an upsized US initial public offering.

item.title: A 50% Surge in Zombie Oil Wells Prompts Texas to Crack Down on Toxic Water Leaks
item.description: Texas is planning new rules to speed up its response to so-called zombie oil wells after the number of sites leaking toxic wastewater increased more than 50% this year.

item.title: Amundi to Run Landmark $3.5 Billion EU Public-Private Bond Fund
item.description: Amundi SA has been chosen by the European Union to run a landmark bond fund that will seek to combine private and public capital to be allocated toward development goals in emerging markets.

item.title: Colombia Hosts Fossil-Fuel Exit Talks as Global Energy Crisis Deepens
item.description: Oil and gas volatility could be a tailwind for the first-ever summit of its kind, which starts Friday in the city of Santa Marta.

item.title: Batteries and Natural Gas Become Unlikely Companions
item.description: In their quest for power, data centers are building gas and battery power off the grid.

item.title: Data Centers Are Finding a Surprising Way to Deploy Batteries
item.description: By pairing energy storage with natural gas, hyperscalers are able to get power faster and do so behind the meter.

item.title: EU Starts Roll-Out of Russian LNG Import Ban at a Difficult Time
item.description: Europe is beginning the roll-out of a ban on imports of Russian liquefied natural gas at a difficult time, with the war in Iran seriously disrupting global supply.

item.title: Climate Change Is Already Showing Up in the Cost of Living
item.description: Economists and central bankers are getting better at linking temperatures to price increases.

item.title: Australia’s Multi-Billion Dollar Undersea Power Cable Given Environmental Nod
item.description: Australia has given environmental approval to a multibillion -dollar undersea power cable project, linking the mainland to renewable power generated on the island state of Tasmania.

item.title: Fail on Climate Action and Miss Out on Promotion, China Warns Officials
item.description: China will begin a major campaign aimed at accelerating climate action by local authorities, warning regional officials that they will be held accountable for failing to make progress toward President Xi Jinping’s green targets.

item.title: MUFG Hires Asia Project Finance Head From Clifford Capital
item.description: MUFG Bank Ltd. has hired Saumitra Shrivastava as head of Asia project finance based in Singapore, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Wheat at Highest Since 2024 as Drought Stokes Food Inflation
item.description: The US’s major variety of wheat jumped to the highest in almost two years on deepening concerns over crop conditions, with about 70% of the production area now under drought.

item.title: World Bank Teams Up With Amazon for a $120 Million Bacon Tree Bond
item.description: The World Bank has teamed up with Amazon.com Inc. on a bond whose proceeds will go toward rehabilitating ecosystems in South Africa’s Eastern Cape Province.

item.title: Energy Storage Is the Latest ‘Not in My Backyard’ Battleground
item.description: The pushback threatens to slow a major battery boom in the US.

item.title: Apple, Amazon Push Back on Stricter Emissions Reporting Rules
item.description: The tech giants and dozens more companies are resisting changes proposed by the Greenhouse Gas Protocol, a global standard-setter.

item.title: Cost to Build a Natural Gas-Fired Power Plant Surges 66%, BNEF Says
item.description: Costs to build natural gas-fired power plants in the US increased 66% between 2023 and 2025 as developers propose building more projects nationwide to meet rising electricity demand.

item.title: Climate Negotiators Seize on Fossil Fuel Crisis Ahead of COP31
item.description: Soaring oil and gas prices are helping to bolster the argument that the world needs to accelerate its move to clean energy sources.

item.title: Toxic Rain Falls on Russia’s Tuapse City in Oil Facility Blaze
item.description: Polluted rain fell on Russia’s Black Sea city of Tuapse as authorities struggle to extinguish a blaze at local oil facilities following a drone attack, highlighting the environmental effects from the war in Ukraine.

item.title: California Is Ground Zero for the Growing Battery Backlash
item.description: Locals worried about fire risks and industrialization are fighting developers looking to build hundreds of energy storage installations.

item.title: China’s March Solar Panel Additions Slow to Hit Four‑Year Low
item.description: China’s new solar installations slowed sharply to hit a four-year low for the month of March, underscoring mounting pressure across the struggling sector.

item.title: In Dakar, a Cultural Center Grows Around a Baobab Tree
item.description: Francis Kéré’s design for the Goethe-Institut Dakar draws on the Burkinabé architect’s practice of using sustainable and climate-resilient local materials.

item.title: UK Finance Unit Sets Up $1.5 Billion Fund for Asia Energy Supply
item.description: British International Investment, the UK’s development finance institution, has created a £1.1 billion ($1.5 billion) fund to back energy transition sectors in India and Southeast Asia, with a view to partnering with pension funds and other institutional investors.

item.title: Vietnam Capital Scales Back Gas Bike Ban on Infrastructure Gaps
item.description: Vietnam’s capital is dialing back plans for a sweeping ban on gasoline-powered motorbikes as gaps in charging networks and public transport force a shift to more limited, time-based restrictions.

item.title: Lessons From One of the World’s Greenest Cities
item.description: Sadiq Khan shares how he fought for environmental policies through a tumultuous decade as London’s mayor.

item.title: China Battery-Maker Gotion Says War Shifts Focus to Clean Energy
item.description: Gotion High-Tech Co. Ltd., a major Chinese battery manufacturer, is seeing a renewed global focus on the green transition as fossil fuel disruptions due to the Iran war drive demand for clean-energy technology.

item.title: Race for Next UN Chief Heats Up With First Round of Interviews
item.description: The race to become the next leader of the United Nations moved into a higher gear with the four declared candidates facing hours of questions over two days. Top among them: can the UN be made effective again?

item.title: How Microsoft Spooked the Global Carbon Removal Market
item.description: In the early days of April, a group of mid-level employees at Microsoft Corp. started making calls that would send tremors through the global market for carbon removals.
